content:
The steam rises from the griddle, but today it does not veil me. It is just water turning to air.

I am Meiying. I say it to the rhythm of the spatula scraping the iron. *Mei-ying.* It is a soft sound, a bright sound, like the light that hits the top of the Transamerica Pyramid while we down here on Clement Street are still swaddled in grey. 

For fifteen years, I thought the fog was something I h
